name: Jaylen Warren
headline: Warren broke out as a lead back, but Dowdle clouds the path
body:
Warren's 2025 was the breakout the advanced numbers had been hinting at: 211 carries for 958 yards at 4.54 per pop, six rushing scores, and an RB18 finish worth 177.1 fantasy points across 16 games. The contact balance is real — 23 broken tackles, 2.36 yards after contact per attempt, and a 10.9% explosive-run rate — and his 0.04 EPA per play flipped positive after two negative seasons. He also stayed busy through the air, turning 45 targets into 40 grabs, 333 yards and two touchdowns while logging 234 routes.

The catch is opportunity, not talent. Pittsburgh added Rico Dowdle, who profiles as a genuine threat for early-down and goal-line work under a new staff, and Warren's 14 goal-line carries from a year ago aren't guaranteed to hold. The receiving floor is his insurance: with Kenneth Gainwell's vacated targets up for grabs, Warren can absorb passing-down volume that props up his PPR value. Draft him as a back-end RB2 with committee risk priced in around the RB24-29 range.
